data.title: KLA, Lam Research receive upgrades on WFE acceleration: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded both KLA (KLAC) and Lam Research (LRCX) to Overweight from Equal-weight due to a likely acceleration in the wafer fabrication equipment market.

Applied Materials (AMAT) remains Wells Fargo's "top pick" among the semiconductor equipment sector.

Lam Research and KLA were up 9% and 6%, respectively, during early market trading on Thursday. Applied Materials was up 7.5%.

"Although semi cap is now a consensus long & has outperformed YTD, avg +20% vs. Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) +9%, we think the group can continue to work as we expect demand data points to suggest a further tightening of logic & memory supply/demand dynamics supportive of a WFE acceleration in 2027," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Aaron Rakers, in a Thursday investor report. "We increase our WFE estimate by an average of 10% & raise C2026 & C2027 estimates to above Street."

Wells Fargo increased its price target on KLA to $1,600 from $1,250 and raised Lam's to $250 from $145.

"We are upgrading KLA to Overweight from Equal Weight as we think the company can continue to drive WFE outperformance by 2 nm momentum," Rakers noted. "We expect positive 2 nm demand dynamics to continue in 2026, in which we expect inspection sample rates to remain elevated as the industry begins to introduce BSPD."

Meanwhile, Lam is expected to outperform due to memory supply and demand dynamics. 

"While we expect Lam to reiterate that its estimated $40B of incremental spending on NAND to upgrade >2XXL will play out over multiple years, increasing tightness in the NAND industry will leave it focused on an acceleration in 2026/2027," Rakers added.

Earlier this week, Stifel said it expects WFE spending to increase by 10% to 15% in 2026, which equates to $10B to $15B in year-over-year growth. The gains will be led by advanced foundry/logic and DRAM.

data.title: Wall Street rallies as chip stocks climb and Trump pauses critical mineral tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street advanced on Thursday as investors reacted positively to President Donald Trump’s decision to delay tariffs on critical minerals, alongside his assurance that he does not intend to remove Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Leading the way higher was the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which advanced 0.9%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) climbed 0.6%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was higher by 0.2%. 

From a sector vantage point, eight of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in positive trading territory, with Info Tech leading the charge. Going in the other direction, Energy has suffered the most so far as oil prices (CL1:COM) are down 4%. 

Treasury yields edged higher across the curve as investors sold bonds. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 3 basis points to 3.55%, while the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) ticked up 1 basis point to 4.14%. 

On the economic calendar front, initial jobless claims dipped by 9K to 198K for the week ended Jan. 10, which was lower than the 215K forecasted level. Also the Empire State Manufacturing Index came in at 7.7 versus the 1.0 consensus level according to data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. 

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) have popped up 3.7%, and Boston Scientific (BSX) fell 4.8%.

data.title: Canadian Solar wins U.S. patent dispute against Maxeon
data.description:
Canadian Solar (CSIQ) +2.6% pre-market Thursday after saying it won a favorable ruling in its patent dispute with Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN) from the Patent Trial and Appeal Board of the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office.

Canadian Solar (CSIQ) said the board's ruling held that all claims asserted by Maxeon (MAXN) related to alleged infringement of patents at issue in the federal court litigation are invalid.

Canadian Solar (CSIQ) denied allegations of patent infringement by Singapore-based Maxeon (MAXN) related to the company's TOPCon solar cell technology.

"This victory not only reflects the fairness and soundness of the international intellectual property protection framework, but also demonstrates Canadian Solar's solid technological foundation and its mature legal capabilities," the company said.

data.title: Allstate estimates sequential fall in Q4 catastrophe losses
data.description:
Allstate (ALL) on Thursday estimated December catastrophe losses at $80M, or $64M after-tax, compared to November's $46M, or $36M after-tax.

However, fourth-quarter catastrophe losses are expected to fall to $209M, or $165M after-tax, compared to $558M in Q3.

Shares were +0.50% pre-market to $197.00.

Protection policies in force increased by 0.2% in December to 38.28M from 38.21M in November.

data.title: ImmunityBio gains as Anktiva powers over 400% Q4 sales growth
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) shares traded higher on Thursday after the company announced that, based on preliminary financials, its net product revenue for Q4 ending December 31, 2025, reached nearly $38.3M, indicating ~431% YoY growth driven by its cancer therapy Anktiva.

Compared to $31.8M recorded in Q3 2025, Q4 indicated a 20% sequential sales growth, the San Diego, California-based biotech said, adding that its full-year preliminary net product revenue reached nearly $113M, implying ~700% YoY growth.

Approved in multiple countries, including the U.S. and the U.K., as a bladder cancer therapy, Anktiva posted 750% unit sales volume growth last year compared to 2024, according to ImmunityBio (IBRX).

Additionally, the company reported $242.8M in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of the end of last year and highlighted the recent approvals of Anktiva for non-small cell lung cancer and bladder cancer in Saudi Arabia.

data.title: Stuhini Cxploration changes corporate name to EraNova Metals
data.description:
• Stuhini Exploration (STU:CA) said on Thursday that it will officially change its name to EraNova Metals with a new ticker symbol 'NOVA', effective January 19, 2026.
  • The name change aligns with EraNova's forward-looking strategy to advance exploration and development across the Ruby Creek Project- which hosts a large molybdenum resource alongside high-grade silver, gold, and emerging copper-gold porphyry targets, the company said.
  • The CUSIP and ISIN numbers for the common shares have also been changed to 29478G106 and CA29478G1063, respectively.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rigetti, Quantum Computing initiated with bullish views at Rosenblatt
data.description:
Rosenblatt Securities began coverage of quantum computing stocks Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and Quantum Computing (QUBT) with Buy ratings.

Shares of Quantum Computing jumped about 3%, while Rigetti Computing climbed around 1% premarket on Thursday.

Quantum Computing (QUBT)

Rosenblatt initiated QUBT with a Buy rating and a $22 price target. 

"Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi) has legitimate quantum assets across photonics, compute, security and sensing as well as burgeoning thin film lithium niobate ((TFLN)) fabs that could supply both their and industry needs for integrated quantum photonics, nonlinear optics and optical waveguides," said analysts led by John McPeake.

The analysts noted that the company is also in the process of acquiring fabs ((LSI)) from Luminar for less than five times revenue that should improve the P&L optics and further diversify their business into another key laser market. With a total of $1.6B in cash (no debt), the company's interest income is currently more than their operating expenses, according to the analysts.

"We like the risk-reward here with an immediate opportunity to participate in quantum computing, a fab-driven chance to see their current products put on chips and acquire customers in the fast growth optical communication sector, a view into developing a photonic gate-based quantum computer, and finally the possibility to drive quantum security deep into everyday consumer edge devices," said McPeake and his team.

Rigetti Computing (RGTI) 

The firm started coverage of Rigetti with a Buy rating and a $40 price target. 

"We like Rigetti's modular approach to qubit scaling and their internal fab approach. Although error rates and timelines are a bit of an issue right now, we are giving them the benefit of the doubt relative to the postponed Cepheus 99.5% two qubit gate fidelity target in 1Q. Last week, the company announced that their modular 108 physical qubit Cepheus-1-108Q system would be a few months late as they were having issues with their two-qubit gate error rate of 99.0% vs. their target of 99.5%, but the company just (1/13/26) reiterated their ability to resolve the issue by the end of 1Q," said McPeake and his team.

The analysts noted that superconducting peers IBM (IBM) and privately held IQM have reported 99.92% and 99.85% two-qubit gate error rates respectively, so Rigetti does need to get its error rates down to be more competitive.

In addition, the analysts said that, though still in development, superconducting peer D-Wave (QBTS) is heading towards potentially even lower error rates after the acquisition of Quantum Circuits.

The analysts said that Rigetti has its own quantum processing unit ((QPU)) fab, an installed base of 18 machines (6 at customers and 12 internally and exposed on the cloud), and a compelling way to network multiple QPUs together via tunable couplers.

"Finally, the company's work with leading quantum error correction ((QEC)) partner Riverlane is promising over the medium to long term," said McPeake and his team.

data.title: Philip Morris plans to make more U.S.-focused investments
data.description:
Philip Morris International (PM) highlighted on Thursday that the company is strongly committed to investing in America, with over $20 billion of investments made since 2022, the majority of which were noted to be made to acquire and further invest in U.S. manufacturing capabilities, commercial rights and infrastructure, and U.S. jobs.

"We’re investing in the country’s future, starting with accelerating the shift to a smoke-free America, a nation free from cigarettes," stated Philip Morris U.S. CEO Stacey Kennedy. "Being fully invested in America begins with making better products and leading an industry transformation responsibly, but it extends far beyond that. Guided by innovation, a sustained focus on impact, and a deep commitment to the communities where we live and work, we’re creating high-quality, high-skill jobs and strengthening the places that power our workforce," added Kennedy.

Looking ahead, Philip Morris (PM) expects to make substantial additional investments in manufacturing, operations, and people, further supporting U.S. jobs and legal-age nicotine consumers.

data.title: Caledonia Mining plunges on upsized note offering, lower Blanket Mine production
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (CMCL) -17.4% pre-market Thursday after announcing a private placement of $125M in convertible senior notes due 2033 to institutional investors, upsized from $100M, and reporting "nearly identical" production in 2025 as in 2024.

The company said it plans to use the debt primarily to fund development of the Bilboes project in Zimbabwe, which would be the country's biggest gold producer.

Caledonia (CMCL), which already operates the 80K oz/year Blanket mine in Zimbabwe, plans to develop the Bilboes mine at a projected total capital cost of $584M, including $132M this year to launch development.

Production from the new mine is expected to begin in late 2028, with steady-state production of 200K oz/year expected from 2029 for an initial period of 10 years.

At the Blanket mine, Caledonia (CMCL) said output totaled 76,213 oz of gold in 2025, in line with its 75K-79.5K guidance but down slightly from 76,656 oz in 2024; in Q4, gold production at the mine fell 12% Y/Y to 17,367 oz, hurt by lower tonnages from higher grade areas and interruptions in the electricity supply at the end of the quarter.

For FY 2026, Caledonia (CMCL) guided for Blanket production of 72K-76.5K oz, with best results in H2 as higher-grade areas increasingly come on stream.

Cost guidance for FY 2026, with all-in sustaining costs at $2.1K-$2.3K/oz sold, reflects higher direct operating costs at Blanket compared with the first nine months of 2025, primarily due to inflation and increased costs, the company said.

Caledona (CMCL) also said COO James Mufara has left the company.

data.title: Genesis Energy declares $0.18 dividend
data.description:
• Genesis Energy (GEL) declares $0.18/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 4.34%
  • Payable Feb. 13; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • See GEL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
